Comment.value: This translation flow is designed for Title Translations
Script is called from Trasnlate button in Title Localisation Card Window when translation method = "Human"

Stage Name Text status HT Text status MT 1. Language added - (text does not exist) - (text does not exist) 2. Text prepared for translation Not translated Not translated 3. Original text sent Not translated Sent to API + async translation id:¶[id_from_api] (async only) 4. Translated text imported Not reviewed Not reviewed 5. Translation in review In progress In progress 6. Translation approved Approved Approved
For Human translation - localisation will be in STAGE 3 after this script if STAGE < 3 when triggered
----------------- Intitiate all variables from Localisation that will be used in process ----------------------------------

Calculation.Text: Case ( IsEmpty ( FAT Localisation::TextfieldsCreationTimestamp ); GetAsTimeStamp ( "2000-01-01" ); FAT Localisation::TextfieldsCreationTimestamp )

Comment.value: ------------------ Check that all export paths are in order for human translation -------------------------------------

Calculation.Text: User X Title @ Title::Title Filepath export="" And FAT Localisation @ Package::Translation method = "Human translation"
"You haven't defined an export path. Go to Title settings, select an output folder and redo the export."
User X Title @ Title::Title Filepath export

Comment.value: ------------- Check if texts needs to be arranged in a new sort order ---------------------------------
------------- Returns a boolean AND timestamp when last original text field was created ---------------------------------
------------- If texts needs to be arranged in a new sort order - Arrange text ---------------------------------

Calculation.Text: "Text needs to be arranged in order"
"Before starting the translation, texts needs to be arranged in the right contextual order for the reviewers. This might take some time."

Comment.value: User pressed Stop - Add event to log

Calculation.Text: "Texts needed new arrangement. User stopped. Last text created " & $last_original_textfield_creation_timestamp & "."

Comment.value: Title and Package texts now are in contextual order
If no errors - save the timestamp when finding new texts

Calculation.Text: "Texts were successfully arranged in contextual order."

Comment.value: Something went wrong when arranging texts. Maybe a record is busy.

Calculation.Text: "Something went wrong when sorting the original texts in contextual order.¶Probably there's someone else working with the title right now.¶¶Do you want to stop the process or continue?"
"User stopped because an error occured when arranging texts in contextual order. " & Get(ScriptResult)

Comment.value: User pressed Skip - Add event to log

Calculation.Text: "Texts needed new arrangement but user skipped. Last text created " & $last_original_textfield_creation_timestamp & "."
"Please, enter the name of the translation provider that will be used for this translation. ¶¶This will make it easier to backtrack the translation later. "

Comment.value: ---------------------- Stage 1 - Localise text fields - Duplicate new texts ---------------------------------
New strategy opening a separate FAT Text file to increase performance
For 40 text fields time reduced from 9 sec to 3 sec using FM Server 20.3.1 with Loop Flush: Defer setting
With previous server version with Loop Flush: Always - time was probably longer.

Calculation.Text: "Error when creating new text fields for localisation"
"Something went wrong when creating new localised text fields.¶Probably there's someone else working with the title right now.¶¶Do you want to stop the process or continue?"
"User stopped because error when creating new localised text fields. "
"Error when creating new localised text fields but user continued process. "

Comment.value: Update Localisation with date when localised text fields were created.
The commit below is needed for allowing PSOS to make changes
Send all texts in Title in one bunch
Write JSON file
Make sure all PSOS changes are viewed in window
